title: Database Node RAM Exhaustion Causing Query Failures
symptoms: MySQL process killed by OOM killer on analytics node
System memory at 128GB/128GB with 0 swap available
Multiple concurrent queries with large temporary table allocations
Analytics dashboard queries returning connection refused errors
InnoDB buffer pool consuming 96GB plus 30GB in temporary tables
root_cause: Multiple concurrent analytics queries with unbounded temporary table sizes exhausting all 128GB of RAM on the analytics read replica, triggering OOM killer
resolution: Restarted MySQL process, implemented query memory limits using MAX_EXECUTION_TIME and tmp_table_size configurations, and staggered analytics report scheduling
preventive_actions: Implement per-query memory limits for analytics workloads
Stagger analytics report scheduling to prevent concurrent heavy queries
Add RAM utilization monitoring with early warning at 80%
Configure swap space as safety net for temporary memory spikes
Implement query admission control limiting concurrent analytical queries to 5

timeline.event: MySQL process killed by OOM killer on analytics node
Analytics dashboard alerts fire for query failures
Rajesh Kumar investigates, confirms OOM kill event
Memory profiling reveals 23 concurrent heavy queries
MySQL process restarted with reduced buffer pool

embedding_summary: MySQL analytics read replica RAM exhaustion from concurrent analytics queries with unbounded temporary table allocations consuming all 128GB. Analytics dashboards unavailable for 40 minutes. Resolved by MySQL restart, implementing query memory limits, and staggering report schedules.
